title: oxalis calviniensis
intro: Oxalis calviniensis, a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, is a type of tuberous perennial native to South Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of oxalic acid, which has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Oxalis calviniensis has been used to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, acting as an antibacterial agent, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies through various methods, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
